They are still dealing with some of the fallout from the corporate spying in the first book and a suspected murder has them thinking that their troubles aren't over yet.Shana's dad also has a continuing storyline with his new lady friend. I loved his conviction that her blindness doesn't matter. I really liked his conversation with his daughter about his feelings for her late mother and the difference in his feelings now.One of the most enjoyable parts of the book is Dalton's search for the woman that he met in a bar one night. Dalton is the brother who is the biggest player of them all. He's never had to work for a woman's attention and it seems to have gone to his head. When he tracks down the mystery lady he's surprised when she wants nothing to do with him. He has an even bigger surprise in store when he finds out who she is. His book should be very interesting.

1 starDNFed @ 40 something %Too many things bothered me here to give this book more than 1 star.I had high hopes for his one because the synopsis sounded really good and I like Jackson because she gave meDuty or Desirewhich I absolutely loved.However.This one was a mistake.At least to me.Why?1.First off, it'snot a standalone.It's a continuation of the first book in the series.Why didn't I read it then?Well, I didn't want to.Why again?Because I have this rule, thatif passionflix decides to turn a book into a movie, it's a 100% guarantee I will hate not only the film but the book, too.So in other words, it's sort of an indicator of what books I should steer clear of.I've learnt that after many painful attempts of trying to get through some of the 'productions' or the 'books'.That truly was an agonizing lesson to learn.2.We quickly learn thatthe main characters, Shiloh and Cadenhad known each other their whole life before they started dating. He is a famous musician and she opens her winehouse or sth when we meet them. And in the pastthey were about to elope but something happened and they ended up separate.So what happened, one may ask?If I understood correctly, after Caden's mother was murdered a long time ago,Shiloh's fatherforbade her to contact the family. And when they secretly reunited, discovered they were soulmates and decided to marry, her father had different plans and decided to meddle. Hesent Caden some fake pictures of her with some dude and so our dumb hero assumed she was cheating on him.And just like that, he refused to talk to her or listen to her explanations.The thought that it could be untrue or it might've been a photoshop never even crossed his dumb mind.So apparentlytrust was not something they built their relationship onsince he refused to even give the girl the benefit of the doubt.Better,he started sleeping around and made sure she knew about all the bimbos he fucked while they were separated.And once I learnt all that, my interest in how this story would go dropped to zero.Because I'm sorry but a guy like this doesn't deserve a second chance.End of story.3.The Grangers' father, who back in the day was accused of murdering his wife and sentenced for 30 years,Sheppard, is now in fact in prison.BUT WHAT A FANCY ASS PRISON THAT IS!Why?Well, perhaps due to the fact that Shep spends most of his time meeting different people right and left. Physical contact included.Also, with a constant access to internet he keeps in touch with everyone and everything.He can check his emails, hire people and even romance his attorney.No questions asked.I wouldn't be surprised if he called for his favourite take out and had it delivered to his 'cell' every now and then.4. The constant switching between the charactersand their povs makes the entire story confusing and boring.5.Theheroine is a complete and utter doormat and she takes that idiot back at around 35 %into the book.He didn't even have to grovel.Or apologise.Why?Because he was hot and she missed him.Her words, not mine.If that's not weak and pathetic, I seriously dunno what is.I could continue the list but what's the point?I didn't like anything here.It was confusing and annoying. And maybe I would've kept reading had the couple been at least a bit appealing or showed me some signs of intelligence.That didn't happen, however.Hence the 1-star rating.

I thoroughly enjoyed this book, hence the 5 out of 5 rating. It is the second book in a series, but it contains enough details so that you'll know what's going on and get caught up in the story. There's a romance, naturally, and an ongoing mystery that continues through the series. I highly recommend. Kudos Ms. Jackson!Read my full review (and check out other reviews) at:Romance Between the Covers

Title: A Man's PromiseAuthor: Brenda JacksonPublisher: Harlequin MIRASeries: Granger #2Reviewed By: Arlena DeanRating: 5Review:"A Man's Promise" by Brenda Jackson..."A man's word is his bond. His family is his strength. His heart is his own.Superstar musician Caden Granger has spent years running from love, commitment and family. Yet despite his fame and fortune, he knows the kind of respect and adoration he needs can only come from one person—the very woman who wants nothing to do with him.Corporate fund-raiser and society maven Shiloh Timmons finally got her life on track once her relationship with Caden ended, and she's in no hurry to revisit a romance with the man she left standing at the altar.If Caden can't have Shiloh by his side, all the success in the world will mean nothing. Now he has a chance to renew his promises…but is it too late?"What I enjoyed about the read...I loved it and found it a continuation of the first series...'A Brother's Honor.' I would definitely suggest you read the first series to truly understand what is going on in the second series. "A Man's Promise" was mainly of Caden Granger and Shiloh Timmons love story and it was a real interesting one that will bring to us 'lies, misunderstandings, secrets hurt and betrayal.' Now add some mystery, suspense, romance, drama and conspiracy into all of that and you have one amazing story that just keep on going into the next series. ..Dalton's story. I don't want to give too much away just know that the Granger brothers...Jace, Caden and Dalton will give you a such a intensed read from start to finish. By picking up 'A Man's Promise' you will find that this author has done a excellent job giving us a well written novel from start to finish with all of its twist and turns. The characters were all simply off the chart ...well wait a moment...their were a few to shake you head at...but mostly all were well developed, well portrayed, colorful and so believable only helping to give the reader a well attention grabber and oh yes I loved that ending...a cliffhanger. This was very well done. The only thing left to say is I will be waiting for the next series.. about Dalton and I am sure there will be even more for us... unlocking and tying it all together in this who, what, when and why of it all in this last series.

This is the 2nd book in the Granger Series, the story of Caden Granger and Shiloh Timmons. In keeping with the storyline of A Brother's Honor, Mrs. Jackson gives us a story of passion, love. Intrigue and revelations. The love these siblings have for each other as well is very endearing.Caden and Shiloh have a complicated history filled with pain, anger, hurt, misunderstandings and some revelations. We also get a glimpse of how there is a connection with Shiloh's father as and the things that occurred in their families past. Caden is the Alpha male (stubborn & protective). When the truth finally was revealed about their parting, he should have gone about begging her :) Sometimes when you love someone you have to go through emotional changes and loose things to prove how strong love can be.The interaction between the secondary characters made the story that much better. I can't wait to read Dalton story and for things to come to an explosive ending in book 3. I highly recommend this book!!! 5 stars of praise for Mrs Jackson.

Last year I got the chance to read the first book in this series, A Brother's Honor and really enjoyed it. When I saw A Man's Promise on Netgalley I was excited when I got accepted to review it. This series consist of three brothers, the first book was about the oldest brother, A Man's Promise is about Caden, the middle brother and the next book will about the third brother. But the thing is, you need to read the books in order because the books connect to one another.I've read quite a few books by Brenda Jackson, but I have to admit the romance in this book wasn't one of my favorites. It wasn't the highlight of the story for me. I loved the romance in the first book, but I couldn't really connect with it in this one. It might be because I never really had much interest in Caden to begin with, but I wanted to get his story anyway. Caden is a musician. He and Shiloh dated in their past and came close to being married, but Shiloh's dad did everything he could to keep them apart. One moment the two went from hating each other to some bad happened and now their back in love. I couldn't connect with it. It wasn't terrible, but it just fell flat.The highlight of this book was the mystery surrounding the brother's mother. Their father has been wrongfully accused of murdering their mother and has spent the last 15 years in prison because of it. But the boys are determined to find out who really murdered there mother. We found out bits and pieces of information, but not enough to solve the case. We also got some shocking information at the end that left some anticipation for the next book, but we won't find out more until the next book and I'm excited for it.I also like how we got Dalton's pov sometimes in this book (the youngest brother). The whole thing between him and Shana's sister is adorable and I can't wait to see his story in the next installment. But another thing that I didn't care for with this story is the pov from Shana and Jules' father. I sort of feel it's pointless. It isn't really that important to me that he's found love. It's sweet and all, but sort of irrelevant to the actual story itself. Overall, the book was okay. It had suspense from all the mystery, but the romance fell flat for me.

Destined!!!A Man's Promise is the second book in the Granger Series. The 3 brothers are brought back together when their grandfather who raised them is on his deathbed. He is all they have since their mother is dead and their father is in jail for her murder.Caden and Shiloh were close as kids but after the murder of his mother, Shiloh was forbidden to have any contact with him by her father. Shiloh and Caden would reconnect again and built on their friendship from childhood. They fall in love and decide to elope and spend the rest if their lives together. Shiloh's father gets wind of the plan and puts one of his own in action. This plan would separate them for years.Shiloh learns the truth about her father's deception after his death. Her first thought is to tell Caden but he is still to hurt by what he believes is her betrayal. Her mother eventually tells him the truth in an apology since she believes he has already been told the truth. Once he learns the truth, he becomes determined to win her back. Shiloh is not feeling him right now and is determined to stay away from him. Yet, we know when a real man makes a promise there is nothing you can do to stop him.Caden is determined and works his way back into her life and that does not sit well with a lot of people. Love is truly on display. As people continue to plot to stop the brothers from digging into their mother's death, more clues and people step in to protect them.This book is definitely a great read and page turner. Brenda Jackson has captivates us again. You will have your own clues and suspects for sure.

Let me start off by saying Brenda Jackson did that. If I could give this book more than 5 stars I would. The sexy love scenes Caden & Shiloh is just wow. The suspense and drama and all the twist and turns kept me interest to the point that once I started reading it I could not stop. Down side is I'm sad because the next book does not come out until June 2015 (Dalton's story) I love me some Dalton, he kept me laughing to the point of tears in this book. The story is so heartfelt. Caden and Shiloh have had been best friends that was torn apart but found each other again. Shiloh's evil father manage to tear them apart again. But they come back together and this time Caden promise's nothing will tear them apart again. I really don't know how I feel all my emotions came into play. I LoVE THE GRANGER MEN!!! This is a must read. I know I will have to read both books all over again. Check out a Brother's Honor too.

I guess I'm in the minority, because I didn't love this book. Not at all. I've read almost all of Brenda Jackson's books and enjoyed them, but this Granger series is just not doing it for me. I started reading this book months ago, and would come back to it every now and again, but it has never drawn me in. Since Book 3 is coming out soon, I decided to finish this one so I'd be ready for the next one, but it was truly a struggle. The premise of the series is a good one, but the story feels painfully dragged out, and there are so many characters to keep straight. And the dialogue seems so stilted...everybody talks in such a formal manner.I usually love Brenda Jackson's suspense stories, because they truly keep you guessing until the end. I would love to know who killed Sylvia Granger, because I think it's gonna be a big shocker, but not sure that I can make it through another book.

I stopped almost half way through. EVERYBODY wants Shiloh to understand Caden's POV but when she tried to tell him the situation, on MANY occassions, he treated her like the worst person EVER. After he finds out that she wasn't cheating on him but was in the hospital fighting for her life and grieving over losing their baby, he mounts a full court press to get her back and he is dumbfounded when she doesnt want to talk to him, go out with him, or see him. I stopped reading the book because I knew she was gonna forgive him within 2 months and without him redeeming himself.

Caden and Shiloh FINALLY find their happily-ever-after in this second installment of The Granger's series. The journey for the Granger brothers is far from over as they attempt to uncover the mystery behind their mother's murder in order to free their father from prison. NOW I am impatiently waiting for Dalton's story.
Great Story, the suspense is killing me. Can't wait to find out who is actually the killer, hope I don't have to wait to long for the next book.

Caden and Shiloh are my favorite!This is my favorite of all the Granger books. The way Mrs. Jackson describes Caden Granger’s love for Shiloh, the intensity and fierceness of his love for her takes my breath away. Caden is willing to do anything to make up for how he hurt Shiloh and it’s so beautiful to witness. Shiloh stands her ground about moving on from Caden, but the drama that unfolds leaves no room for distance between them. I. Just. Love. Caden and Shiloh!

After reading the first book in this series I felt compelled to read the second, because of the murder/mystery that spans both tomes. I am very happy that I didn't stop after the first book, because the series has evolved into an extremely exciting and tantalizing storyline. I can hardly wait to begin the next book in the series.👍🏾🔥👍🏾🔥👍🏾🔥👍🏾🔥👍🏾🔥
This was very good
Lacking on the steamy, however A Man's Promise makes up for that in spades with a compelling plot.
Another twist in the mystery! I must get the next book immediately!

Book 2 of the "Granger Series" was so damn Brilliant!!!!!! Another stellar story by author extraordinaire Brenda Jackson.This is middle brother Caden's story, world renowned saxophone player, Grammy winner and every other music award. Along with his two brothers Jace and Dalton, they promised their dying grandfather that they would all come back together to help rebuild the family business, which they had no idea was on the brink of bankruptcy. Their dad Sheppard Granger has been in jail for the past 15 years for the murder of his wife (which he did not do). The truth is out there somewhere but Shep's refusal to have his case reopened with a new team of attorney's doesn't make a bit of sense, except he knows that whomever killed his wife, will go after his three grown sons.Caden has everything he has ever wanted, except for the love of his life Shiloh Timmons. A story about two childhood best friends who would become lovers and soulmates, for only the unthinkable to happen, sending everyone into a tailspin of heartache, bitterness, regret, brokenness and unforgiveness. In the last story, Shiloh goes to Caden again to tell him what happened all those years ago, only to be turned away again but this time, she will not come back to be humiliated by him again.For Caden what he was so dead set on being the ultimate betrayal by the only woman he has ever loved was too much for him, he wanted nothing to do with Shiloh, he didn't even want to hear her name or ever see her face. But an unlikely visitor, a bombshell confession shatters everything that Caden believed and in a race against time, he will try and recapture the one woman who very names makes him inhale and exhale. But will Caden be too late? Has the damage been done to the point were it is irreparable? Well you will just have to read it to find out.Here we get to see Jace and Shana (Book 1), Hannah who is like the boys mother, Shep who is still in jail but has a secret love himself (Go Shep Woot! Woot!), and a team of hidden bodyguards sworn to protect and keep the Granger men safe. There are deadly secrets that the townspeople are willing to kill for, betrayals within families, so called friends and murder. Book 2 has definitely been kicked up a few notches so please hold on to your seat belts or better yet strap yourselves in because this is going to be a crazy ride.The scene stealer for me is Dalton Granger, he is the youngest, extremely smart and a billionaire but is funny as heck. Dalton will have you rolling on the floor all the while looking at your Kindle/Nook saying (did he just say what I think he said LOLL!!!!). Dalton Granger's story is next and his book is "A Lover's Vow" and this will be the final story for "The Grangers" series.Well Until My Next Review Ciao Bellas!

Is a promise always kept? Is love always and forever? Can you be convicted for a crime you didn’t do? Is a brother’s honor and a man’s promise things that will keep you going and believing in true love. These are questions I wanted to know when I read this book by author Brenda Jackson. Well, I would say the answer is YES to them all.In this book, A Man’s Promise, Caden Granger and his love interest Shiloh Timmons is in the spotlight with their drama and romance. Their off and on relationship pulls you in to where you want it to end with a happily ever after. In the meantime, you are confronted with other characters in the book that have equal love stories but not so much drama – whew! Beautiful, beautiful love stories that touched my heart. (smile)Now, don’t get to comfy because this is a scandalous story so lurking in the background is who murdered Caden’s mother (Sylvia Granger) and sent his father to prison to serve a 30 years jail sentence for it. While getting to the answers or solving the murder, Brenda Jackson introduces many more flawed characters with shady backgrounds all back dropped in a small town called Charlottesville, VA.Let me back up a little for you, it all started with a grandfather who was on his dying bed summoned his 3 grandsons (Jace, the oldest, Caden the middle child and Dalton the youngest) who he helped raise when their mother was murder and father found guilty for her murder went to prison. He asked the three brothers to help the family business, Granger Aeronautics, to get back on top of the aerospace game winning government contracts while helping free their father from prison too. Big Job!!! – But someone has to do it – why not family members – right?Trust me when I tell you this is a great book to read. It can be a stand-alone book but if you really want to uncover the real story and solve the mystery faster, then read book 1 “A Brother’s Honor first then read this one. However, if you are good sleuth then by all means dive right into this story with book 2. Since this is a trilogy Mrs. Jackson had to save the big WOW for book 3 – Dalton’s story. I know we are in for a treat because you get a sneak peek at his crazy antics in this story. It should be funny, witty, scandalous, conclusive and all so sexy. (Wink)

What can I say Mrs. Jackson have done it again, A MAN'S promise Is the second book In this trilogy. A BROTHER 'S HONOR is the first book in this three book trilogy. If you haven't read the first book yet I would advise to read that first, because if not you have miss a great book. This is a series about three brothers who was grown and had gone out into the world leading their own lives until their grandfather had an heart attack. The brothers had to return home to take care of their grandfather, but he didn't make it, but they had maked a promise to their grandfather to return home to run the family business. Now here is where every thing start happening OH I forgot to tell you that the father is in prison, because he was supose to have killed their mother when they was young boys. The father is in prison, for a murder he dibn't commit, the grandfather died from a heart attack they say and some one is trying to take over the family business now if that isn't suspense, mystery and cospiracy. This is Caden Granger and Shiloh Timmons love story, Caden and Shiloh have been in love with each other for years, but there are people who always tryed to keep them apart. Ever after Shiloh father died some one is still trying to keep them apart. Okay here come the good part some one is trying to kill Caden in this story, In A Brother s Honor some one was trying to kill Jace, Caden older brother. I guess Caden and Shiloh love story will have to wait, because this have really got good now. I want to know who killed Sylvia Granger, who killed the grandfather and why, and what group is that who wants Sheppard to stay in prison. Just stay tune it have really got good now.

The second in a series, it took me a while to get all the characters straight and to keep them that way, although several were introduced in the first story in this series. Caden is one of three sons of Shepperd Granger, in jail for the murder of his wife. His sons believe he is innocent, but have not looked into finding out who killed their mother because their dad has convinced them not to.In this story, sandwiched around the romance of Caden for Shiloh, we learn that the boys' mother, NOT the father, was having an affair and that she might have been doing more than one man other than her husband.Several people the boys, now men, have trusted are now suspect, including Shiloh's own father and maybe her brother, who is not happy when she elopes with Caden. But, his band manager, also tries to kill him to cover gambling debts with the million dollar insurance policy he took out on Caden, the sax headliner in the band Granger manages.Who killed Mrs. Granger will probably be revealed in the story that focuses on Dalton, who already has the hots for the PI hired by Caden to find out who tried to run him down. I was disappointed not to find out who the bad people were and why they seem to be targeting all three Granger brothers and others in their circle of friends and acquaintances. Obviously, the author wants me to read the next book. I'll try to hold back my frustrations until it becomes available.

No matter how hard people try they cannot keep true love apart. Caden Granger and Shiloh Timmons have been friends since they were younger. This friendship soon turns into something more but after the death of his mother they are torn apart by Shiloh father. Though they believe they were apart for two different reasons neither party knows what has truly happened to break them apart. When her father dies Shiloh learns the truth of what her father did but still refuses to go to Caden with it even though she loved him. Steaming from what he believes is betrayal Caden isn't looking for love or anything that comes with it plus someone is trying to kill him. With all this going on Caden soon learns the truth and that he wants nothing more than to have Shiloh by his side. Those granger man you have to love them. The second book in a trilogy "A Man's Promise" is definitely worth the read. I recommend you read the first granger story "A Brother's Honor" but this book can stand alone. Love, honor and commitment is definitely what a woman wants and each book so far has given us just that. This books deserves 5 stars if you have not read it go get it NOW. Mrs. Jackson you did again hats off to you.

This is the second of a three book series about three brothers whose lives were forever changed when their father was wrongly convicted of their mother's murder. When their father is sentenced to 30 years the brothers are raised by their paternal grandfather who calls them homewhenhe suffers a major heart attack. The brothers make a deathbed promise to their Grandfather to save the family company and prove their father's innocence. Middle brother Caden takes center stage in A Man's Promise when he is forced to face the one true love of his life whom he believes betrayed him in the most hurtful way. Ms. Jackson weaves a beautiful story of rekindled love and mystery. The clues as to who is trying to ruin Granger Aeronautics and why Sheppard Granger is still serving time for a crime he did not committ take several twists and turns. A wonderful bonus are the blossoming love stories of other key characters and the hillarious setup of youngest brother Dalton's love story. Do yourself a huge favor and read A Brothers Honor to get ready for A Man's Promise. I can't wait for Book 3 ~~ it is gonna be a blockbuster!

There are three books about the Grangers, each with one brother as the hero. The brothers did not work in the family company but each followed his dream. Having promised their grandfather on his deathbed to take over managment they try to discover the mole in their company. Solving this mystery goes on during the three books, and the culprits are revealed in the last chapter.This book is a stand alone in regard to romance, each brother finds his match.Caden and Shiloh's story is a second-chance-romance.I am a stickler for timelines and those are just terrible in each book.Caden's secret body guard is on the phone with his employer when he gets busted by Caden. His boss listens in and sends some collegues and an attorney for support and to explain various issues, all the while Caden and the bodyguard nearly shoot eachother and get into fistcuffs. But they don't do any harm because said collegues and the attorney arrive within five minutes. Even at night with empty streets it takes some time to get from downtown to a gated community which I picture to be a little bit outside.The writing is questionable, too many food analogies.

Angela Whigham Less than a minute agoThis is the second book in the Granger's series. Their mother was murdered and their father wrongly convicted of the murder. The brother's were raised by their grandfather, before he died he call them all home and they made him a deathbed promise to save the company.Superstar musician Caden Granger has spent years running from love, commitment, and family.Shiloh always held Caden's heart and he was always the man for her. Lies, misunderstandings, secrets, hurt and betrayal. Caden makes Shiloh a promise.....A Man's Promise to win back her love and trust and put her back in his life where she belongs.Mrs. Jackson gives you a little insight on Dalton's the youngest brother, relationships and what you can expect from his story.There are so many twists and turns in this story that have you on the edge waiting on the next installment to see what happens. So pick up A Brother's Honor if you haven't already, then read A Man's Promise.

As the saying goes, good things come to those who wait. After reading A Brother's Honor I was anxiously awaiting the next installment of the Grangers! I was pulled in with the detail and intrigue. The clues are popping up, the plot thickens and every where you turn there is love, love, love. Caden and Shiloh's story drags you in, you feel apart of the action. This is getting bigger than one couple. The story is well written and the supporting characters are string enough to stand alone. Not to give too much away but the heat and friction ignite like sparklers in the night sky. Caden is man big enough to admit when he's wrong and smart enough to fight for what he wants. Shiloh has been hurt, is she willing to risk her heart again? You have got to get this book and if you missed the first in the series run to your nearest store and pick up a copy or better yet download to your e-reader :-)

The Saga Continues….Caden Granger was well worth the wait! Caden and Shiloh were once in love and planned to marry despite her family’s disregard for the Granger family. We learned Shiloh doesn’t show for the wedding in “A Brother’s Honor” and Caden doesn’t handle it well. Once Caden understands that he misjudged Shiloh and their split was a result of family interference, Caden is determined to win her back. Caden makes a promise to regain Shiloh’s love and trust. The phrase on the back on the book sums it all up - A man's word is his bond. His family is his strength. His heart is his own. I loved Caden’s determination to right his wrong and Shiloh’s strength to not make it easy for him. In the midst of their new beginnings, A Man’s Promise is filled with more secrets, mystery and suspense. Who is trying to kill Caden? Is it connected to their mother’s death? I can’t wait for the conclusion of the Granger saga!

I love this book, part 2 of three about the Granger brothers. Caden is the sexy musician of the bunch. His story is of a lie that slow to come to light from an unlikely source, However, Caden goes about righting the wrong after a few years.However, the bigger mystery is who killed Caden's mother over 15 years ago. His father is serving time for a crime that has left the young men scarred but determined. Jace, his older brother shares a secret with their younger brother Dalton. Dalton has quite a few ups and downs in this sequel to "A Brother's Honor". However, his story is coming...Mrs Jackson knocks it out of the park with installment of the Grangers. Being a Virginian, it gives me pride to hear about all the wonderful parts of Charlottesville told in this story.

This has been the motto for Jace, Caden, and Dalton Granger since their father was put in jail for the murder of their mother fifteen years ago. As adults they are trying to save Granger Aeronautics, keep a promise to their grandfather, look for love and stay alive.A Brother's Promise and A Man's Promise each gives you just enough romance, drama, mystery, and mayhem to keep you turning the pages asking for more. The books leave you with a few answers, more questions, and the shock value has you waiting on book 3.I don't think this series will stop with three books because I for one want to know about the other sons and Carson. All my friends get your copy of A Brother's Promise you will enjoy.

This is the second book in the series and it's as good as the first book. Caden Granger and Shiloh Timmons were more than sweethearts four years ago, they were to meet in Los Vegas to get married but Shiloh never showed. Now Caden is back to help out with his families business and he finds out Shiloh is back in town also. When he finally finds out why she didn't show in Vegas they talk and bet back together.The mystery that surrounds the business and their fathers imprisonment continues in this book with some issues coming to light.I can't wait for Dalton's book because there is so much anger there.

book.title: Huddle With Me Tonight
book.author: Farrah Rochon
book.description: Originally Published by Harlequin Kimani Romance – September 2010Entertainment columnist Paige Turner is known for getting under the skin of the people whose books she reviews on her popular blog. When her criticism of NFL standout Torrian Smallwood’s cookbook/memoir goes viral, it sparks an online war between the city’s most beloved blogger and one of football’s hottest stars.Torrian couldn’t care less what some book blogger thinks about his upcoming memoir. His main focus is on making a success of the new restaurant he’s opening for his sister. That is, until he meets the gorgeous Paige Turner face-to-face. When a local TV station invites them to bring their online battle into the kitchen in a reality TV cook-off, Torrian comes up with a new goal: making Paige his.Will either of them be able to stand the heat, or will they succumb to the white-hot attraction that neither can deny?Reading order:Book 1: Huddle With Me TonightBook 2: I'll Catch YouBook 3: Field of PleasureBook 4: Pleasure Rush

reviews.text: What a totally yummy romance! There is so much tension between the main characters, and their banter goes from angry to sexy fairly quickly, too. I loved the character development, but I loved the longing even more.

4.25 Stars / 3 Steam FansSuch a fun, entertaining read. Paige and Torrian start as rivals that give into a special love and partnership.Video review available in Week 24: Jun 11 – Jun 17, 2023, weekly book reviews on Steph's Romance Book Talk (https://www.youtube.com/@stephsromanc...)

I don’t remember reading this before which is fine because I have now read it and it’s SO GOOD. Torian, a football player on the NY SABERS who may have to say goodbye to his career soo. and Paige, a columnist who isn’t too shy to share her opinion, are a fun, cute couple that start out as enemies and after being pushed together for publicity, become much more. This is was an entertaining intro to the Sabers series and I already have book 2 queued up! So many thanks to one of my faves for giving me something good to read during my vacation.

Audiobook. Library. 3.5 rounded up. I didn’t love this as much as her other series but it was sweet and fun and fairly low angst. Given the premise I wanted them to be more angsty with each other but overall it was enjoyable.
I enjoyed it but them liking each other was too much at times.(this is as far as I got into the review but I've read another book since them)3.5 to 3

Football and romance what a combination!Yeah my two favorite things rolled into a perfect package hot sexy football player and a beautiful love story filled with blush worthy love scenes who could ask for more. Definitely a series worth reading and as always Ms. Rochon never disappoints her fans🎉😍👍

Paige is a popular culture reviewer on a newspaper's blog; Torrian is a football player publishing a celebrity memoir/cookbook prior to the opening of his sister's restaurant. They clash over a negative review she writes of his book, and a local television station decides to exploit that clash by hosting the pair in an on-air cooking competition.Huddle With Me Tonightis a buoyant, enthusiastic romance. The sex scenes include laughter, which is always a positive in my book. The characters are grown-ups who may act foolishly--namely, in the set-up, in which they get wrapped up in a battle of ill-advised Internet comments--but who realize when those actions are beneath them, and they work hard to be better and to take care of one another. The title's apt because it's once the two characters get over their initial, shallow conflict, they're super-cooperative, intent on mutual success, and are on one another's "team."The ending was too rote for my tastes (a misunderstanding and refusal-to-communicate leads to separation leads to realizing they can't live without one another leads to groveling and reunion, etc.; a lack of motivation for a sudden antagonist), but it still gets four stars for how I kept rereading some of the scenes where the two characters are kind and caring to one another.Fans of the earlier Julie James books might like some of the dynamics here: an assertive and professional heroine, a sweet and famous hero, a showdown between the two, and depiction of good friendships outside that relationship.

I just didn't connect with this book. There was no real conflict, everything seemed to come easy, and the ending was horrible.The hero is cruel to the heroine for the last-minute break-up, and she takes him back way too easily. Also, the whole bit at the end where she is rethinking her career because she made some reviews based on what she thought the audience would like rather than being honest. She talks the ENTIRE book about how honest she is, how she just puts it out there, shooting straight and not embellishing for "ratings," and suddenly she's realizing she has been doing it all along? WHERE WAS THIS IN THE BOOK? It's never even HINTED at until the hero dumps her, and it just left me boggling. If she's been lying to herself all along, it would have been great to get a hint of it. We had Angela there as a friend/boss, and she could have mentioned it ONCE, to give some foreshadowing. But no, we just are to assume the heroine's been lying/denying all along. Meh.This would have been a 3-star book up until the end.
Really enjoyed this one but the ending was a little abrupt, I wish there had been an epilogue!

I’m a fan of Farah Rochan’s series “The Boyfriend Project,” so when I saw this series recommended on a list of Football Book Boyfriends at the start of the football season, I knew I had to take a listen.Paige is a columnist (book reviewer and food blogger) for a local NYC paper. She reviews the memoir of Saber’s football star Torrian, which released ahead of the opening of his new restaurant - which he purchased for his sister. Paige says the book is trash. Torrian gets Twitter fingers and comments back on the review. The two get into an all out war on the internet. It’s a classic enemies turned lovers.My major issue with Torrian is that he talks a lot about how he’s a good guy, but idk if readers actually experience it. It’s a lot of “I don’t usually do this,” but meanwhile he keeps acting a fool and being a hot head.The storyline was interesting. There was one pejorative about people who use and are addicted to substances, which was uncalled for and felt out of place in the storyline. The book is old and the people are just starting to understand substance abuse, so I didn’t DNF at that point, but it did piss me off.Themes: trust, medical conditionsRounded up to a 3/5. I’ve read better book boyfriends.

Loved ItHot hero meets sexy blogger. I loved the chemistry between them! This is a great love story, with very hot sex scenes, and memorable characters. It definitely made me want to read the next book in the series.
Rated 3.5 StarsHuddle With Me Tonight was cute, sweet and sexy. I enjoyed it but I wasn't a fan of how the conflict was resolved. That said I'm looking forward to reading the rest of the series.
There’s something about her writing style that isn’t for me…The romance was developed well but there wasn’t enough sports in the “sports” romance.
I love Torrian. Like, completely love him. Paige is funny, snarky, and oh so loving and they are perfect together! I can't wait to read more from this series!

I thinkHuddle with Me Tonightis a very well-written and unique romance novel. This is my first time reading a Farrah Rochon novella and afterHuddle with Me Tonight, I'm definitely interested in finding other novels by her. The book kept me entertained, I loved all the characters, and the story was quite different from your typical romance novel (this statement comes from a young woman who has read (maybe) 15 romance novels in her lifetime but fully intends to read many more -- take it with a grain of salt if you must). I give it 5 stars because the characters were like able, the story entertaining, and the writing is pretty darn good.

this story is exactly what i needed. super sweet hero who happens to a be a hunky football player, totally lovable heroine who happens to be an entertainment columnist with a Halle Berry haircut. both had understandable issues and back stories that didn't make me want to stab my eyes out. cute sex scenes that made me giggle. likable friends and family on the side. i will definitely read the next book in the series.also i didn't know that Harlequin even had this line. thanks Twitter.
I enjoyed the story, but felt that it wrapped up to neatly at the end. It seemed as if the author was looking for a way to quickly end the story. Still it's a good read.

Picked this up as a Kindle deal earlier this year and finally got around to reading it. I thought I would love it for two reasons: 1) I enjoyed the other two books by Farrah Rochon that I've read, and 2) because I really enjoy football so I was excited for a sports romance. This book was only okay for me. It was a quick read but the writing wasn't really there so I wasn't drawn in, which is a shame.Basically, I didn't feel the supposed chemistry between Paige and Torrian. I liked how they danced around one another early in the book but didn't really understand why they got together. The Act 4 breakup was trite and silly, as both characters themselves admitted.Paige had the potential to be a total badass but, she wasn't. Like, was she totally out for her career or no? Because at the end of the day, I wished the book had been at Paige and her career, rather than a romance...I doubt I'll be reading the others in this series, which is a shame. Instead of reading this, readThe Dating Playbookby the same author.

Football and blogging when they collideOne thing I appreciate about Farrah 's writing and plot development is that they evoke realism. It's not all fairytales and Roses. When you read Torrian and Paige's story you can image in present context this taking place. Because we all know the entertainment industry cross sectors and hate it or love it, opinion blogs exist and keep us abreast of our favorite entertainers.So what makes Paige so special is you can see yourself. She's determined, opinionated, strong willed. Torrain is athletic, dedicated, supportive family man who has charm for days. With the two, you smile . You just know that sparks are to come.You'll enjoy the story simply as someone who can appreciate what new love is when career and ambition comes in the way and the need for balance is inevitable.

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆Paige is a writer for Big Apple Weekly, reviewing books and restaurants and events. When she reviews NFL athlete Torrian’s new book, part memoir part cookbook, badly, he’s furious, worried about how this will affect his new restaurant. His older sister brought him up and this restaurant is being opened for her. The online rivalry causes real life sparks to fly between Paige and Torrian, generating all the heat 🔥𝗠𝘆 𝘁𝗵𝗼𝘂𝗴𝗵𝘁𝘀This earlier story of Farah Rochon’s is set in NYC, featuring a humble writer who suffered reading problems when she was younger, the down to earth professional athlete who is determined to win her over. Both characters are well known in their community but they are able to share their deepest feelings with each other. It’s a sweet read with a splash of spice added 🌶️Read on KindleMy rating 4/5 - ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️

It's decentWe don't really get to know them. There is more hinting at the small details or dealing with the after effects of a meltdown than there are hits on a football field. The emotional and unique stuff are why books are better than movies. I don't want a montage of good scenes or to ignore a dramatic crying jag. I want to know that he unexpectedly laughed and beer went up his nose. I want her to laugh so hard she snorts. I want her to have a universal or individual revelation while balling her eyes out. I want to see him actually go a little crazy because he can't stop missing her. Make it real and it doesn't have to be all good. I can be utterly wrecked by a book and still love it.

Honesty is the best policy ....There is something to be said about a man who recognizes that he has found his match. These two got off to a rocky albeit honest start where she reviewed his book and he didn’t like what she had to say. Naturally things evolved and through a good hearted televised competition the tables turned. The hook was well written and flowed very well. I felt however that the ending was very abrupt as if the author just gave up. There were lots of developments we learned about in the last few pages and then boom, done.... hopefully we can find out a little more about Paige’s career choices as we read more in the series. It’s definitely a head scratcher.

I enjoyed reading this novel. Matter of fact, I am glad that it appeared as I was downloading another novel. It is a heartwarming, cheek-reddening, romance novel. A football player that is respectful of himself and to women, and a writer that tells it how it is gets into an argument from a review Paige wrote about Torian's cookbook.The book kept me entertained. I didn't want to put it down. Though, I expected a steamy sex scene, the sex they did have didn't take away from the novel. They are a cute couple that bickered back and forth until Torian finally decided to reach out. It's just different from a lot of the romance novels I've read.This is the first novel I've read from this author. I just downloaded the rest of the series. I am excited to see what those are aout.

The was a really cute enemies-to-lovers with banter and hot, sexy looks. It did something really well that I normally don’t care to read: a male character that keeps trying even when rebuffed..I didn’t completely love it because the third act breakup hit one of my auto-DNF-buttons: someone assumes the worst…and doesn’t give the other person even a chance to explain. There are satisfying apologies for the first part of that, but part two is left unresolved..It’s a testament to how engaging the story was that I kept on going long enough to even get to the apologies; I have a long TBR and DNF frequently. If miscommunication is not a big deal for you, this is a great, great little story.

Good BookI like how magnetic Torrian and Paige were from the beginning and even through their word sparring match they still desired each other. I'm glad that when they hit a snag and broke up that Paige forgave Torrian and got back on track. I like how Torrian looks out for his sister Deirdre and nephew Dante since she put her life on hold to raise him when their parents died. I wonder why Deirdre wouldn't give Theo a chance. I like how close Torrian, Theo, Cedric, and Jared were as teammates and how close Paige and Angela are. I can't stand Barry Stark and I hope he gets his in one of the next books.

I have been reading this author's newer books, so wanted to check out her back list. Sadly this one didn't work for me. I liked Torrian, liked how he was trying to branch out of football, thought the cookbook restaurant was a good tie in. I didn't care for Paige, she came off as mean. I also felt like she knew she did something wrong because when she ran into him she gave him a different name. Of course Torrian did make the stupid move of making a comment on the review. At this point I realized I didn't care what everyone thought of this move or if these 2 could get past things, this one just wasn't for me.

So we all know I love a good sports romance and this one did not disappoint!We have Paige, a book review columnist, and Torrian, an NFL football player with a new cookbook out about to open a restaurant. Paige’s career is in the up and up with her column about to be syndicated when she writes a scathing review of Torrian’s book. They start an online battle which turns into a cooking competition and of course they get together haha.I loved their banter and their characters in general. The tension built was fabulous! I wish we had gotten more than a snippet of the cooking show though and the ending was wrapped up abruptly.Staci McIntyre

Paige’s career is in the up and up with her column about to be syndicated when she writes a scathing review of Torrian’s book. They start an online battle which turns into a cooking competition and of course they get together haha.I loved their banter and their characters in general. The tension built was fabulous! This was really cute. Torrian was so charismatic lol. I felt like DUH Paige was smitten. I loved their instant chemistry because honestly it was believable lol. But I aint appreciate how he aint bother to investigate who leaked his info before jumping to conclusions!!!! Insane actually and idk how Paige forgave him so fast lol. But I did like the tussle between author and reviewer. With all the tussling on booktok lol it was nice to see that interaction and see an author take the criticism and roll with it. And I admired Paige for standing her ground on her views. Honest reviews matter if we want to continue to get quality books! Torrian was a good sport for that as well.

Great ReadI read one of the New York Sabers books when they were first published years ago, but never finished the series. I am glad this series has been rereleased so that I can read the remaining books. This was a wonderful story about Paige and Torrian. What made it special was how it portrayed the main characters as strong individuals that cared about family and community. It is always nice to read stories where athletes aren’t portrayed as idiots and journalists are all shown as sharks. Nice read.

book.title: Forever Yours
book.author: Francis Ray
book.description: The last thing Victoria Chandler wants is to get married… again! Now the threat of financial ruin jeopardizes not only the Fort Worth beauty's hard-won independence but her chain of lingerie boutiques as well. In desperation, Victoria seeks help from wealthy Kane Taggart. There's just one catch: she has to become the wife of the seductive, silver-tongued rancher… for one year.What could possibly happen in twelve short months?Plenty… especially when Kane counter-offers with an irresistibly indecent proposal of his own…

reviews.text: A warm marriage of convenience romance from 1994 that didn't feel overly dated (aside from the hero's mustache, honestly). Like a lot of MoC stories, the premise deserved a very strong eyeroll, even though the characters definitely needed the push. I liked both of them, but there is a very defined dynamic to this one: the hero has had feelings for the heroine for years and in the present recognizes that this is love very quickly, and the heroine is the one who goes through all the character growth and needs time to grow (and then to recognize) her trust and love. Ray's writing style is engaging; I laughed out loud a couple times.ETA: Content warning for abuse in the heroine's backstory.Victoria was previously in an emotionally abusive marriage, mentioned very early in the book; we find out in the last third of the book that it involved marital rape, and that prior to the events in this book, the sum of her experience with sex has been that marital rape. She eventually resorted to financially bribing her husband to stay away from her.

This the first Francis Ray I've read and it was a somewhat nice introduction. Ignoring the preposterous way the book startedwith Victoria being given an ultimatum by her grandmother to find a husband and get married or lose her storesone could be forgiven for thinking it would go downhill from there. Fortunately, it did not and I enjoyed the slow and steady build up of trust that had to happen between Victoria and Kane for their relationship to work. I also enjoyed their banter.

Awesome bookBecause I like only hers and wanted the other books I didn't like the way Stephen made Victoria feel while married to her and after they divorced but I like that Kane helped her believe in love again
This novel by Francis Ray was the first Black American romance novel I read. The experience was fantastic and I am still reading and supporting 26 years later.

A good ole sweet romance 🥰Victoria is a divorcee who has sworn off men but her grandmother gives her an ultimatum to bring home a husband or have her business taken away from her. With the help of her best friend, Bonnie, she is able to reach an arrangement with Bonnie’s cousin, Kane Taggart but the thing is, Kane has always loved Victoria.We see how Kane sets to loving the elusive Victoria into total surrender. For all lovers of “marriage of convenience/arranged marriage” tropes, here is food.This is my first book from this author and I enjoyed it!
Love This BookI read this book years ago. I had to get the digital version for my kindle. Victoria and Kane are so good together. I love how Kane didn't give up on her. He was just what she needed.
3.5 stars
This was one of my first novels and it has been a part of me since!
Loved it! I read Heart of the Falcon years ago and wanted to go back and read the other books in the series.
I am so happy my friend introduced me to Francis Ray! 🩷

Forever Yours was a quick, cute kind of fake relationship turned real. Victoria owns her own lingerie boutique, at least until her grandmother threatens to call in her loans if she doesn't remarry within the month. Victoria is recently out of a bad marriage, so she looks to hire someone to be her husband. And who better than her best friend's cousin, whom she knows from their teen years? Kane is a sweet guy and agrees to Tori's out-there scheme, but he does want more than she's willing to give.I liked Forever Yours, but it was pretty slow for being so short. I could understand Tori's hesitance in getting close to Kane after her previous marriage, but I did wish she'd change her mind quicker! She was also a kind of boring character without much personality. For being a lingerie seller, there was no spark! I was expecting a lot more from her in that regard. The dialogue was also a bit awkward in places, but this is a pretty old book. Overall, it was an enjoyable read which used one of my favorite tropes. I just wish there had been more to it.Read more of my reviews atPinkindle Reads & Reviews.

Victoria divorced from an abusive marriage. She opened Lavender and Silk lingerie shops. Ones she is most proud and worked her hardest for. Until, her grandmother threatened to take them away if she doesn’t remarry in the next month. Tori’s best friend, Bonnie, introduced her to her cousin Kane.Kane is the one that drove through a storm just because bonnie n her were scared. He was the one that held her close to himself so she could feel save. With one look, Tori remembered how caring he is, but will she stop being so selfish and open up to him? They both say they want something different but at the end they both have the same.I loved this book it’s very sexy and blood pumping. This book is with someone with patience because it’s very slow paced but to the point where it don’t give away. I recommend this book to anyone. It’s a very understandable book.

I love when an author creates relate-able characters. Victoria "Tory" Chandler is a woman burned badly by a previous relationship. Unfortunately, she's backed into a financial corner by a well-intentioned but overstepping grandma who wants her precious grandchild to marry again. Enter Kane Taggart to the rescue. Physically imposing & smart, Kane's never forgotten the young lady he saved from a storm years ago.I'd read this story again & again. Who wouldn't want their own Kane Taggart?

its a little outdated with the language but timeless all the same. It was different type of theme a girl needing to save her business via marriage for a year and its kind of "year right" but it was still interesting.

This is the first book I that I read by Francis Ray. I was hooked from the beginning. I loved Kane and Victoria. Kane was patient and went after what he wanted. Victoria finally realized how wonderful Kane is and the series was off and running!

Victoria is the most annoying person! And exactly why was she going to loose her business? If her grandparents were rich why didn't they just pay whatever she needed? I also do not care for the grandmother.
March 30,2010
Really good read.
This was definitely a great start to this series. The pages flowed easily it was a very good read.
love FR's books... this was a beauty
I didn't recognize the cover but, I own this book. Just a different version. I always love a novel by Francis Ray. I want to own all her works.
Such a heart felt Love story!
Great readtHis is my first Francis Ray experience; it won't be my last. Loved it.Definitely would recommend to anyone. Wonderful.
It is always great and read some stories again...this is most definitely one of them!
This one wasn't my favorite, but I love the books by Francis Ray. The Grayson Friends & Taggart Brothers series are great.
For NCIS fans--anyone else think the guy on the cover looks like Director Vance on the show?
This was the first book by Francis Ray that I read.

book.title: Any Way You Want It
book.author: Maureen Smith
book.description: Zandra Kennedy owes the success of her elite escort agency to some simple rules. Her girls offer companionship, not sex. And business always comes first. Zandra won't allow any man to rule her life the way her father dominated her mother. But that doesn't mean she's immune to fantasies—especially when it comes to her childhood friend, gorgeous former navy SEAL Remington Brand.For years, Remy has been breaking other women's hearts while guarding a secret. He's in love with Zandra's feisty spirit and the vulnerability she keeps hidden under those lush curves. A Caribbean vacation leads to an erotic encounter that's every bit as mind-blowing as they'd imagined.Remy wants more. But he has another secret, too—a betrayal that could shatter Zandra's career, their lifelong friendship and a passion too explosive to deny.…

Ortada kalan bir sürü kardeş var.Maureen duy sesimizi.. Yazmazsan olmaz yani :D
